---
title: "Lecture 38: Spring Boot Configuration, Annotations & Build Systems"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "March 26, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 38]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Spring Boot Configuration, Annotations & Build Systems]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 12 | Unit V: Spring Boot<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Explain why Spring Boot is preferred over classic Spring setup
- Configure app settings using `application.properties` and profiles
- Use core Spring Boot annotations correctly
- Compare Maven and Gradle build systems
- Structure a Boot project using clean package conventions
- Use actuator basics for environment and health visibility
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Why Spring Boot"}

[Why Spring Boot?]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Auto-configuration reduces boilerplate
- Embedded server (Tomcat/Jetty/Undertow)
- Starter dependencies simplify build files
- Production-ready defaults with actuator support
- Quick setup for REST and microservices
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Project Structure"}

[Recommended Project Structure]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```text
src/main/java/com/example/app/
  Application.java
  controller/
  service/
  repository/
  entity/
src/main/resources/
  application.properties
  application-dev.properties
  application-prod.properties
pom.xml or build.gradle
```

::: {.body-text-s}
Keep `Application.java` at top package so component scan covers subpackages.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Main Annotations"}

[Core Spring Boot Annotations]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

| Annotation | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `@SpringBootApplication` | Enables auto-config + component scan + config |
| `@Configuration` | Bean definition class |
| `@Bean` | Registers method return as bean |
| `@ConfigurationProperties` | Binds external config to POJO |
| `@Value` | Injects individual property values |

---

## {data-menu-title="Properties Config"}

[application.properties Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.properties}
server.port=8081
spring.application.name=task-service
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/taskdb
spring.datasource.username=root
spring.datasource.password=secret
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
logging.level.com.example=DEBUG
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Profiles"}

[Environment Profiles]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.properties}
# application-dev.properties
logging.level.root=DEBUG
spring.jpa.show-sql=true

# application-prod.properties
logging.level.root=INFO
spring.jpa.show-sql=false
```

```bash
# Run with dev profile
java -jar app.jar --spring.profiles.active=dev
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Configuration Binding"}

[@ConfigurationProperties Demo]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1|3-6"}
@ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "app.mail")
@Component
public class MailProps {
  private String host;
  private int port;
  private String sender;
}
```

```{.properties}
app.mail.host=smtp.example.com
app.mail.port=587
app.mail.sender=noreply@example.com
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Maven vs Gradle"}

[Build Systems: Maven vs Gradle]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

| Feature | Maven | Gradle |
|---|---|---|
| Format | XML (`pom.xml`) | Groovy/Kotlin DSL |
| Learning Curve | Easier initially | Higher initially |
| Performance | Good | Better with incremental builds |
| Convention | Strong conventions | More flexible customization |

---

## {data-menu-title="Starter Dependencies"}

[Starter Dependency Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.xml}
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

::: {.body-text-s}
Starter artifacts include compatible transitive dependencies automatically.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Actuator Basics"}

[Actuator Essentials]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.properties}
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics
management.endpoint.health.show-details=always
```

```text
/actuator/health
/actuator/info
/actuator/metrics
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes to Avoid]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Keeping secrets hard-coded in `application.properties`
- Using same profile settings for local and production
- Placing `Application.java` in wrong package (scan misses beans)
- Managing dependency versions manually for Spring starters
- Exposing all actuator endpoints in production without security
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Summary"}

[Summary]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- Spring Boot accelerates development with opinionated defaults
- Profiles keep configuration environment-specific and clean
- Build tools and starter dependencies reduce setup friction
- Proper structure and configuration are key for scalable apps
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 39 - RESTful Web Services: Controllers, Request Mapping, CRUD APIs
</div>
</div>
